title: Single-source the TyVar flex-child query in `MLF.Binding.Children`
problem: `src/MLF/Binding/Tree.hs:163` and `src/MLF/Binding/Validation.hs:419` still each carry the same `tyVarChildFilter` + local `collectBoundChildren` wrapper + `boundFlexChildren` body for the direct flexible-child query. That logic is structural binding-tree plumbing, not a separate thesis policy, and its duplication is now stale because `src/MLF/Binding/Children.hs:1` already owns the generic child-scan loop. It also leaves the code slightly out of sync with the existing consolidation note in `implementation_notes.md:1341` that says bound-child collection was centralized.
why_this_is_simpler: Add one shared helper in `src/MLF/Binding/Children.hs:1`, e.g. `collectBoundTyVarChildrenWithFlag`, and route both `Tree.boundFlexChildren`/`boundFlexChildrenUnder`, `Tree`’s `orderedBinders` child scan, and `Validation.boundFlexChildren` through it. Keep binder-existence checks, quotient/canonicalization, and caller-specific error-context strings where they already live. That makes `MLF.Binding.Children` the real single owner of the TyVar child-selection logic while keeping the round tightly bounded to one helper cluster.
why_thesis_exactness_should_be_preserved: The primary thesis passages only require that elaboration/query code identify the type nodes flexibly bound on binders/gen nodes (`papers/these-finale-english.txt:13547`, `papers/these-finale-english.txt:13731`). The duplicated code does not encode an extra paper rule; it merely filters binding-tree children to `TyVar` nodes with `BindFlex`. If the round preserves the existing caller-local binder checks, quotient handling, and ordering/canonicalization behavior, then it changes ownership of the enumeration helper, not the thesis semantics. The primary thesis text is sufficient here; `papers/xmlf.txt` does not appear necessary for this decision.
expected_benefits: Removes the last duplicated TyVar-flex-child query logic from `Tree` and `Validation` without widening the refactor.
Ensures `checkNoGenFallback` and the public binding query path rely on the same structural helper, reducing drift risk.
Brings the implementation into better alignment with the existing binding-helper consolidation story already documented in the repo.
risks: If the shared helper accidentally absorbs binder-existence checks or alters error-context strings, existing failure-mode tests could change unexpectedly.
If the round expands beyond TyVar child enumeration into `boundFlexChildrenAllUnder` or quotient logic, it stops being the intended low-risk simplification.
required_validation: Add a focused source/behavior guard proving `src/MLF/Binding/Tree.hs` and `src/MLF/Binding/Validation.hs` no longer define local `tyVarChildFilter ::` / `collectBoundChildren ::` helpers.
Run `cabal build all && cabal test` before calling the round complete.
